name: Dark dimension (Montero-Vafa-Valenzuela)
summary: A swampland-motivated proposal that a single mesoscopic extra dimension of size L ~ 10^-6 m exists, populated by a tower of light Kaluza-Klein gravitons. The dark dimension's size is tied to the smallness of the cosmological constant via the distance / duality conjecture; the KK tower provides a natural dark-matter candidate (KK gravitons) and predicts deviations from inverse-square gravity at sub-mm scales.

addresses_puzzles.note: Distance conjecture relates the small Lambda to a long compactification length. Predicts dark dimension L ~ Lambda^(-1/4) M_Pl^(-1/2) ~ 10^-6 m, where Newtonian inverse-square law breaks down at the threshold of current short-distance gravity tests (Eot-Wash).
KK gravitons in the dark-dimension tower can be the dark matter; specific abundance calculation by Gonzalo-Montero-Obied-Vafa (2022, 2023) gives Omega_DM ~ 0.25 in the natural mass range.
Bulk fermions propagating in the dark dimension naturally produce small Dirac neutrino masses via volume suppression; aligns with the expected scale.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicted dark-dimension size ~10^-6 m sits just below current Eot-Wash sensitivity (~52 mu m); next-generation short-distance gravity experiments are direct tests.
KK-graviton DM has gravitational coupling only; direct detection cross sections far below LZ sensitivity.

new_fields: KK tower of gravitons + bulk fermions
introduces.note: Dimension size and 5D Planck scale; both fixed by the distance-conjecture relation to Lambda.

history: Montero, Vafa, Valenzuela (2022), 'The Dark Dimension and the Swampland', JHEP 02, 022 (2023), arXiv:2205.12293. Gonzalo, Montero, Obied, Vafa (2022), 'Dark dimension gravitons as dark matter', JHEP 11, 109 (2023), arXiv:2209.09249. Active research program connecting string-theory swampland conjectures to phenomenology.

references.citation: Montero, Vafa, Valenzuela, 'The Dark Dimension and the Swampland', JHEP 02, 022 (2023)
